mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production index signals expanding manufacturing activity that translates into higher corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month acceleration in consumer price inflation above a critical threshold forces the Federal Reserve into aggressive tightening, raising discount rates and crushing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL 3-month change exceeds 2.0%, or Rank IC is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and decreases in nonfarm payrolls engineer a stagflationary drag on corporate revenues and margins that is stronger than either signal alone, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising FEDFUNDS and falling PAYEMS fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered observations, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) over main effects.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, an increase in the unemployment rate signals that restrictive policy is breaking the labor market, triggering a risk-off repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During identified monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month increases in UNRATE are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or Rank IC > -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects accelerating consumer spending that boosts near-term revenue surprises for cyclical equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive month-over-month RSAFS changes do not significantly exceed zero (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer prices are rising rapidly while the federal funds rate remains low and unchanged, deeply negative real rates compress real discount rates and equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and flat-to-down FEDFUNDS does not produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations, or the interaction term lacks significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a rapid freeze in construction activity and household formation that precedes broader demand contractions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns following HOUST declines greater than 5% are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 100,000 workers signals an abrupt deterioration in labor income and consumer confidence that depresses aggregate demand expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative PAYEMS shocks below -100k are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.03.
